At 4% depth — roughly the first transformer layer of 26 — the model has just integrated positional encodings with token embeddings, so its representations are dominated by local co-occurrence statistics rather than sentence-level meaning. Attention heads at this stage are resolving tight bigram and trigram patterns: "frontend developer," "years' experience," "tech stack," and "coffee chat" each activate strong collocational priors that begin orienting the residual stream toward professional-register text, while the capitalized tokens "LinkedIn" and "Stripe" trigger early named-entity signals that haven't yet been resolved into semantic roles. The sentence-final "?" is already nudging next-token distributions toward response-initiating tokens, but at this depth the model has no stable intent classification — it's essentially completing low-level syntactic housekeeping before mid-network circuits can integrate the full cold-outreach frame.

At 4% depth — roughly layer one of twenty-six — the model is almost entirely in embedding and local co-occurrence space: the tokens "You," "seem," "interesting" are being processed as a greeting-plus-compliment bigram cluster, while "What's," "last," "book," "read" are triggering strong co-activation with a question-frame schema anchored to the interrogative contraction. The period boundary after "interesting" signals a two-utterance structure, and "Mine was" followed by a quoted title is beginning to resolve as a self-disclosure template that mirrors the question's second-person framing back to first-person. Competing early activations include a weak social-small-talk path (the opener could precede many topic types) versus a stronger intellectual-exchange path driven by "changed your mind," but at this depth neither wins decisively — the model is essentially still laying down positional and syntactic scaffolding before semantic intent disambiguation can sharpen in the middle layers.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the embedding for "Americans" has fired the canonical "My fellow Americans" political-speech template — one of the most overrepresented opening phrases in political training data — and the first self-attention pass is forming local syntactic links: "our" binding backward to the collective subject, "crossroads" attracting high-salience representations as a pivot-framing noun that predicts incoming contrast structure. The bigram "red and blue" has collapsed into a partisan-politics cluster, but "Not between" introduces a negation that the model's earliest circuits cannot yet fully arbitrate, leaving competing activations for both partisan-conflict and post-partisan-unity frames. Tokens "child," "mom," "library," and "coach" are being pulled into a concrete-exemplar register strongly correlated with motivational rhetoric, while "zip code" is beginning to accumulate socioeconomic-inequality signal that will compete with the optimistic framing established by "hope" and "fair shot."

At 4% depth — roughly layer 1 of 26 — the model has passed tokens through the embedding matrix and one transformer block, so attention is operating over very local windows with minimal cross-sentence integration. The opening tokens "Okay," "so," and "I" are immediately activating strong informal-register and first-person-anecdote priors, biasing next-token distributions heavily toward spoken-style continuations like "figured," "realized," or "just." The phrase "my dog stares at me" is being parsed as a subject-verb-object dependency with a human-animal dyad frame beginning to form, while "when I'm on the toilet" introduces a situational subordinate clause whose bathroom-setting semantics are tokenized but not yet semantically weighted against the humor register. Competing shallow predictions at this layer likely include both mundane pet-behavior explanation and comedic punchline structure, since "finally figured out why" is a resolution frame that co-activates both possibilities before deeper layers can adjudicate.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is resolving token-level syntax and encoding the first wave of distributional priors. The opening bigram "Dearly beloved" fires an extremely high-confidence prediction toward the canonical continuation "we are gathered here today," and indeed that phrase arrives in full, locking in a wedding-ceremony register with near-certainty — the model's next-token distribution over the rest of the opening is extremely narrow, expecting names, union-language, or officiant formulas. The proper nouns "Becky" and "Tom" are being encoded as co-referential participants with asymmetric roles (subject of celebration vs. agent of the anecdote), and the quoted title "The Princess Bride" is partially chunked as a fixed lexical unit. At this depth there is early tension between the high-formality ceremonial frame established by "gathered here today" and the informal anecdote register signaled by "once tried to impress," though the model lacks the depth to fully resolve which frame will dominate the discourse arc.

At this shallow depth, the model has registered the high-salience anchor token "eulogy" and begun binding it to "mom," activating grief-adjacent and creative-writing-assistance priors before most semantic processing has occurred. The syntactic dependency chain "librarian who hated sentimentality, but she secretly loved" is being parsed as a contrastive structure — the conjunction "but" and adverb "secretly" are already generating competing polarity signals around the character sketch. Constraint tokens like "honest," "funny," "not disrespectful," and "short" are entering early attention windows, but at 4% depth the model has not yet reconciled them into a unified generation strategy — it is essentially holding the request frame open while the character's contradictions ("hated sentimentality" / "bad romance novels" / "stray cat") accumulate as salient features that will later need to be synthesized into tone.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is integrating token embeddings with positional encodings while early attention heads sweep local windows for syntactic anchors. The opening tokens "Brothers" and "sisters" immediately activate strong co-occurrence statistics associated with religious address registers, and "I tell you" fires as a prophetic speech-act construction, priming high probability mass on exhortatory continuations rather than descriptive or interrogative ones. The em-dash in "destroy you—it is here" creates a local parsing tension as the model has not yet accumulated enough context to fully resolve the antithetical structure, while the repeated exclamation "Stand up!" begins pattern-matching to parallelistic oratorical templates. The token "church" combined with "amen" in the final clause strengthens a sermonic genre signal that is already forming from the very first vocative phrase, narrowing the predicted response space toward affirmation and continuation of rhetorical amplification.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is barely past raw token embeddings: attention heads are resolving immediate neighbor relationships, so "Good morning ," and "graduates ." are being grouped as a vocative opener, and the embedding for "graduates" is already shifting the next-token distribution strongly toward commencement-speech vocabulary baked in during pretraining. The colon tokens after "bad news" and "good news" are flagged as rhetorical-list markers, but the three-clause anaphora spanning "Build something weird . Build something kind . Build something that scares you" is far outside any single attention head's effective local window at this depth and remains invisible as a structural unit. The token "tip" near the end carries unresolved ambiguity between financial-gratuity and directional senses; the model has not yet accumulated enough contextual signal to commit, so the embedding still sits near the centroid of both senses in representation space.

At layer 1 of 26 — roughly 4% depth — the model has just passed through embedding and its first attention pass, where the opening tokens `[PRESS`, `RELEASE]`, `FOR`, `IMMEDIATE`, `RELEASE`, `:` function as an overwhelmingly strong genre lock: these formulaic markers saturate early attention heads with a document-type prior that suppresses ambiguity almost entirely, routing activations toward press release schema. The bracketed header and the "FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE" fixed phrase together fire as a single recognizable chunk, so even before the model reaches "Acme Corp," it is already building expectations for a named corporate subject, a transitive announcement verb, and a location phrase. Competing interpretations — news article, regulatory filing, satirical parody — remain faintly alive in the residual stream but are already being down-weighted by the co-occurrence statistics baked into the embeddings for this particular sequence of formal-register tokens.

At roughly layer one of twenty-six, the model is almost entirely occupied with local bigram and trigram statistics: the sequence "thank the Academy" is among the highest-frequency fixed phrases in its training distribution for award-ceremony transcripts, so even at this depth the residual stream is already loading features associated with Oscar-speech register before it has processed more than a few tokens ahead. The opener "My friends" activates address-mode templates and elevates probability mass on first-person rhetorical structures, while the surrounding syntax — subordinate "of course," the appositive "who once talked me out of" — is being parsed for its dependency skeleton rather than its semantics. The token "llama" arriving inside a professional-career frame will not yet register as surprising because cross-sentence coherence tracking has barely initialized; the model is predicting punctuation boundaries and function-word slots, not adjudicating tonal incongruity between "Academy" and "llama farmer."

At 4% depth — essentially the first transformer block after embedding — the model has tokenized the input and each token has received its initial positional-aware representation, but cross-token attention has barely begun integrating meaning across the sequence. The high-frequency cluster "campaign ad script" fires strongly against political and broadcast-writing registers in the embedding space, while "former schoolteacher" and "city council" activate overlapping civic-character schemas that will compete briefly before the warm-biography framing wins out. Hard constraint tokens like "30 seconds," "opens with," and "not attack-heavy" are already encoded as structurally salient — their positional salience signals to upstream layers that the response must be time-bounded, scene-initiated, and tonally positive — but the model has not yet synthesized these into a coherent generation plan.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is primarily resolving token-level surface features into basic syntactic and register signals: the triple repetition of "Alright," the shift to all-caps in "WHO'S READY TO GET LOUD?!" and the imperative "Look around you!" are already being encoded as markers of high-energy performative speech, activating embedding directions associated with exclamation, direct address, and crowd-audience framing. The next-token predictions at this depth are weakly formed but lean toward continuation patterns typical of political rally or motivational speech — the phrase "They told us" primes adversarial contrast structure, and "we couldn't organize / we were too divided" is parsed as a parallelism that anticipates a rhetorical reversal, making tokens like "But," "Yet," or a demonstrative like "This" highly probable as continuations. The tension the model is beginning to track is between two competing framings — civic mobilization and potentially confrontational street-action rhetoric — with tokens like "streets," "organize," "protect," and "family" pulling the representation toward a cluster that spans labor organizing, protest movements, and gang/territorial solidarity, without yet resolving which schema dominates.

At this very early layer, the model has completed its embedding lookups and first attention sweeps across the token sequence, with "eulogy" immediately establishing a high-salience constraint — its dense associations with death, formal composition, and commemorative speech begin propagating through the residual stream and dominate the initial topic signal. The first-person frame "I'm struggling" combined with the explicit request "Can you help me start" registers as a help-seeking construction, and the model begins loosely clustering "gardener," "story for every plant," and "quiet strength" as biographical descriptors, though their semantic relationships remain underspecified at this depth. Competing interpretations between grief-support response and creative-writing-assistance response are both weakly active, with next-token predictions skewing toward sympathetic acknowledgment tokens rather than direct task execution.

At this shallow depth, the model's attention heads are primarily resolving local token dependencies: "TED" strongly primes "Talk" as a near-certain continuation, while the colon tokens following "outline," "Opening," "Body," and "Closing" encode a recurring structural schema consistent with formatted documents and instructional prose. The quoted title "The Unseen Architecture of Trust" is being processed as a noun phrase with "Architecture" as syntactic head, but the unusual modifier "Unseen" alongside "Trust" creates mild competition between abstract-structural and social-psychological domain embeddings that have not yet resolved. The enumerated triple — "cybersecurity, marriage therapy, venture capital" — registers as a deliberately incongruous set with no obvious shared superordinate category, manifesting at this layer as unresolved domain tokens whose reconciliation under the "trust" frame is deferred; meanwhile "renewable resource" is already activating environmental and economic collocations that sit in early tension with the interpersonal-trust register established by the title.

At this shallow depth, the model has completed embedding lookup and its first attention pass is beginning to resolve basic local token relationships: it has registered the informal narrative marker "So I asked," identified the compound noun "smart fridge" as a single semantic unit, and detected the nested dialogue structure signaled by the paired apostrophes around both the question and the fridge's response. The token "Dave" is already drawing disproportionate attention because it appears as a vocative inside a machine's speech, activating strong associative weight toward HAL 9000-style sci-fi address patterns that compete with a simpler reading of it as the narrator's name. Simultaneously, the juxtaposition of "out of milk" with "out of hope" is beginning to register a parallelism that the model's earliest circuits tentatively classify as humor or deflection rather than literal information, though the "uncle at Thanksgiving" comparison has not yet been reached and the full comedic arc remains unresolved.

At this shallow depth — roughly layer one of twenty-six — the model is primarily doing token-level pattern matching and very early syntactic bracketing, with no long-range semantics yet integrated. The opener "Here's to" fires a strong toast-register prior almost immediately, so next-token predictions are already weighted toward celebratory nouns and honorifics; "couple" then reinforces wedding-speech as the dominant frame, pushing activation mass toward congratulatory formulas like "May you" and blessing constructions. Simultaneously, the tokens "IKEA," "instructions," "bookshelf," and "Allen wrench" are activating dense DIY-assembly co-occurrence clusters from pretraining, creating a mild tension at the representation level between the ceremonial-speech schema and the mundane-frustration schema — the model has not yet resolved which register governs the clause, because the humor depends on holding both active at once.

At layer 1 of 26 (roughly 4% depth), the model has completed embedding lookup and positional encoding, and the first self-attention pass is resolving local co-occurrence statistics. The token "vote" is the dominant syntactic anchor — its imperative frame activates bigram expectations around a following beneficiary ("for Sarah Chen"), and the proper noun sequence "Sarah Chen" is being consolidated as a named-entity span rather than two independent tokens. At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is operating almost entirely at the level of token surface forms and immediate bigram statistics, so the opening tokens "We gather here" are already activating strong prior probabilities for ceremonial or collective-address registers — political speech, religious assembly, or eulogy — while the negation "not to mourn" creates an immediate suppression signal that pushes against the most probable continuation "but to pray" or "but to remember," redirecting probability mass toward contrastive constructions. The quoted phrase "an infestation of sentiment" is being parsed as a token boundary event around the single quotes, with the model tagging it as reported speech and noting the semantic clash between the negative valence of "infestation" and the neutral-to-positive "sentiment," a tension it cannot yet resolve into irony or character voice. The closing token "bastard" carries a sharp register shift that the earliest layers can detect through embedding proximity to profanity clusters, though the model has no mechanism yet to reconcile it with the eulogy frame established by "honor" — that disambiguation waits for deeper layers.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is operating almost entirely on surface token statistics and local co-occurrence patterns rather than semantic reasoning. At this shallow depth, the model's early attention heads are predominantly pattern-matching on surface token co-occurrences — "Acceptance," "speech," "Lifetime," and "Achievement" activate dense clusters strongly associated with awards-ceremony discourse, and the immediately following "I'd like to thank" confirms that prediction, reinforcing the ceremonial register. The token "Misdirection" produces a mild prediction mismatch against the expected award-title frame, and the model begins activating competing neighborhood signals — stage magic, rhetorical technique, ironic self-deprecation — without yet having the representational depth to resolve them. The colon-quote boundary signals an embedded direct-speech register shift, while tokens like "upside down," "noticing," and "fake" are starting to weight perceptual-deception feature clusters, though at layer ~1 these activations remain largely unintegrated, sitting as parallel candidate interpretations rather than a coherent semantic frame.

At roughly layer one of twenty-six, the model is still operating close to the embedding surface, activating token-level features that flag this text as civic-political speech: the cluster "city council vote" fires genre signals associated with local-government and protest registers, while the colon plus opening quotation mark primes the model to treat everything that follows as reported direct speech from a single speaker. The repeated pronoun contrast — "they" as outgroup, "we" and "I" as ingroup — begins establishing an adversarial frame at the attention level, but the model cannot yet resolve the lexical ambiguity latent in "signs," which simultaneously activates the protest-placard sense from the political context and a vaguer symbolic-meaning sense; that tension will remain unresolved until middle layers. The phrase "calling the cops" introduces a law-enforcement token cluster that weakly competes with the community-safety framing set up by "kids" and "park," creating an early representational pressure the model has not yet committed to resolving in either direction.

At 4% depth — roughly layer 1 of 26 — the model has just passed token embeddings through a single transformer block, where attention heads are performing their first associative sweeps across the sequence. The opening tokens "Press," "release," and "draft" are already activating strong co-occurrence priors toward formal corporate register: the model's next-token distributions are skewing toward bureaucratic vocabulary like "announces," "effective," "units," and "consumers." The colon after "draft" and the opening quotation mark are being parsed as structural delimiters signaling embedded document content, so syntactic heads are beginning to bracket the quoted section as a distinct span. At this shallow depth, the ironic juxtaposition between "orbital trajectory calculations" and "emotional distance" hasn't resolved — the model sees "space" as a high-entropy prediction site with competing continuations across technical, spatial, and interpersonal registers, but lacks the cross-token semantic integration to detect the humorous collision yet.

At this shallow depth, roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is primarily anchoring on surface-level distributional signals from the opening tokens: "It is with profound gratitude and deep respect that we announce" activates a ceremonial-announcement template strongly associated with formal institutional prose, awards, and commemorative language. The capitalized sequence "Order of the Phoenix" creates a named-entity candidate that the model cannot yet disambiguate between a fictional Harry Potter reference and a genuine medal or honor, producing competing activation patterns that the high-register surrounding context ("conferral," "exemplary service," "transatlantic diplomatic") is already beginning to resolve toward the latter. The date range "2018-2023" and "Dr. Eleanor Whitmore" are being slotted as person and temporal-span entities whose relational roles — recipient, service period — are just beginning to crystallize as the model predicts that a laudatory continuation will follow, likely specifying further achievements or institutional endorsers.

At layer 1 of 26 (roughly 4% depth), the model is primarily resolving token embeddings and establishing the earliest attention patterns across local windows. The tokens "Honorable," "Court," "judicial," "petitioner," "statutory," and the § glyph preceding "3412" are already activating a dense legal-register cluster in embedding space, making formal litigation the overwhelmingly dominant topic signal before any deep contextual reasoning occurs. The subject-verb hinge "We respectfully request" encodes a deferential-institutional speech act that suppresses conversational and generative continuations in favor of procedural-document completions, while the relative clause anchor "which demonstrates" opens a syntactic dependency the model has begun tracking but not yet resolved — creating mild competition between a completion that elaborates the exhibit's contents and one that pivots to a court ruling or response.

At 4% depth — roughly layer one of twenty-six — the model's attention heads are doing little more than binding adjacent tokens into low-level syntactic chunks: "The present study" resolves as a nominal subject, "investigates" fires a strong verb-frame expectation, and high-frequency collocations like "hitherto unexplored" and "novel computational framework" are already activating academic-register embeddings even before any semantic composition occurs. The unusual lexical density — "quantum entanglement entropy," "topological phase transitions," "two-dimensional electron gases" — produces competing domain signals (condensed-matter physics versus computer science) that the model cannot yet adjudicate, so early token predictions are spread across technical-continuation tokens (numbers, Greek letters, method names) rather than converging. The syntactic frame "X investigates the correlation between A and B, employing C" is partially assembled, and the trailing participial clause "employing a novel computational framework that significantly reduces algorithmic complexity" is beginning to pull predictions toward quantitative result tokens — coefficients, scaling exponents, benchmark comparisons — that would naturally follow such a methods claim.

At 4% depth — roughly the first layer of Gemma 3 1B's 26 — the model is embedding individual tokens and beginning to fire the earliest positional and bigram-level associations. The opening trigram "By the grace" immediately activates embeddings associated with formal religious and legal preamble, biasing next-token distributions strongly toward royal or ecclesiastical register; the comma-delimited appositive structure "We, Charles the Third" is being parsed as a subject phrase, but the archaic majestic plural "We" creates low-level tension against the model's dominant prior that "We" precedes a verb rather than a proper name. Tokens like "Royal Prerogative," "hereby declare," and "realms and territories" are each contributing strong documentary-genre signals, and by the time "twenty-first day of June" is encoded, the model has already begun suppressing conversational response strategies in favor of those associated with formal proclamation generation or summarization.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is almost entirely in local pattern-matching mode, processing token-level statistics rather than building discourse structure. At roughly layer 1 of 26, the embedding projections have placed tokens like "transform," "formal," "diplomatic," and "note verbale" into representational neighborhoods strongly associated with register-conversion tasks, while "Hey," "got," "tweak," and "signing" occupy colloquial-register territory. The first attention heads are beginning to bind the imperative "transform" to its object phrase "the following casual text," anchoring a task-framing template where the colon after "verbale:" marks the quoted block beginning with "Hey" as input material rather than instruction. Competing activations around "trade deal changes" and "memo" are already priming both formal treaty-correspondence schemas and commercial-negotiation frames, but the model lacks the depth here to resolve whether the dominant output register should weight diplomatic protocol language or standard international business correspondence.

At this shallow depth — approximately layer 1 of 26 — the model is performing basic embedding contextualization and early positional attention, primarily detecting strong collocational signals rather than semantic reasoning. The token sequence "We hereby certify that" is an exceptionally strong formal-register trigger whose pattern appears consistently in legal, academic, and institutional corpora, so the model's residual stream is already biased toward completion tokens associated with certification language: degree names, candidate identifiers, institutional phrases. The surface form "aforementioned" signals that a referent exists outside the visible context window, creating a mild resolution tension that early attention heads cannot yet satisfy, while "Doctor of Philosophy in Molecular Biology" as a compound noun phrase is beginning to be grouped as a coherent unit through local attention over adjacent tokens. No high-level intent classification or response strategy is forming yet — those emerge in middle layers — but the syntactic skeleton of a subordinate "that"-clause following a performative verb is already structuring which token positions are likely to carry evaluative adjectives, correctly anticipating the coming "exceptional scholarly merit and originality" completion slot.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is performing basic token embedding and positional encoding while the earliest attention heads begin detecting syntactic structure. The opening trigram "In the name" fires a strong prior for formal proclamation or prayer registers, immediately reinforced by "His Holiness" — a two-token honorific that activates high-formality ecclesiastical patterns — and the named entity "Pope Francis" followed by a comma, signaling an appositive construction that the model is beginning to bracket. At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is establishing token-level embeddings and beginning positional encoding for a dense sequence of archaic legal vocabulary — tokens like "undersigned," "deliberated," "covenant," "stipulations," "annexed," "hereto," and "hereof" are already triggering strong distributional signals associated with formal contract registers, pulling activations away from conversational baselines. At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model has just embedded the input tokens and is beginning the first round of attention-weighted mixing. The token "Parliament" immediately activates strong priors toward formal institutional and governmental text domains, and "doth" — an archaic third-person singular — rapidly narrows the register prediction toward either historical legislative prose or deliberate archaism, triggering mild tension between a "genuine historical document" interpretation and a "modern text performing formality" interpretation. The long periodic sentence structure, with its main clause ("Parliament doth resolve") delayed by the subordinate "that this august chamber expresses," is barely visible to the model at this depth; syntactic head-dependency arcs between "Parliament," "resolve," "calls," and "furnish" are only beginning to cohere, while tokens like "unilateral," "deliberations," and "sitting days" are flagging parliamentary-procedural semantics that will constrain next-token predictions toward motion-language completions rather than narrative or expository continuations.

At 4% depth — essentially layer 1 of 26 — the model has just passed through embedding lookup and a single transformer pass, so representations are still dominated by token-level and short-span bigram statistics rather than global syntax. At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is performing localized positional embedding integration and beginning short-range attention sweeps that register high-frequency formal-register bigrams: "cordially invite," "Annual Awards," "white tie." At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is barely past embedding lookup and the first attention pass, so what dominates is positional co-occurrence statistics and surface register detection rather than deep semantic parsing. At layer 1 of 26 (roughly 4% depth), the model's attention heads are performing shallow local pattern matching where the opening token "That" holds competing interpretations — demonstrative pronoun versus subordinating complementizer — before the immediately following "this House" begins suppressing the demonstrative reading and flagging parliamentary-motion register. The compound modifiers "low-maintenance" and "north-facing" are parsed as hyphenated adjective clusters but their semantic content — light constraints, care constraints — is not yet meaningfully composed; the model at this stage is more sensitive to the syntactic signal that two specification phrases precede the head noun "houseplant," priming it to expect a constrained-recommendation schema where candidate tokens like "pothos," "snake plant," and "ZZ plant" are already accumulating residual probability mass, though no single prediction dominates because the constraint space is still being assembled from parts rather than read holistically.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model has completed embedding lookup and begun the first attention pass, where positional encodings are fusing with token identities. The "What is" opener immediately biases the residual stream toward question-answering registers, and the token "square" is already competing between its geometric sense and its arithmetic one, with "root" following as a strong disambiguating signal that collapses the interpretation toward the mathematical operation. The numeral "144" — likely a single token — activates dense arithmetic associations in the embedding space, and even at this shallow depth the model is weakly pre-routing toward a short, numeric completion, with the trailing "?" reinforcing the interrogative frame and suppressing narrative or explanatory response patterns.

At layer 1 of 26, the model has just embedded the input tokens — likely segmented as ["How", "▁do", "▁I", "▁assemble", "▁an", "▁IK", "EA", "▁bookshelf", "?"] — and the first self-attention pass is beginning to propagate very local syntactic signals. The "How do I" prefix is an exceptionally strong procedural-question trigger, and the verb "assemble" is already activating features associated with step-by-step instructional registers; even this early, the model is biasing toward numbered-list or imperative-sentence continuations. "IKEA" is partially tokenized (split across subword units "IK"/"EA"), so named-entity coherence is not yet resolved, though "bookshelf" as a concrete physical object is reinforcing a furniture-assembly frame that will dominate future layers.

At approximately layer 1 of 26, the model is primarily performing embedding lookup and the earliest positional attention sweeps, where token representations for "build," "fails," "kill," "process," and "terminal" are being loaded with their raw distributional statistics before contextual refinement. At this shallow depth, the model has completed token embedding lookup and is running its first attention pass, so local co-occurrence statistics dominate over long-range semantics. The token "shoot" sits in a briefly ambiguous state — its violence-related senses carry non-trivial weight until "photo" two tokens later begins propagating a disambiguation signal through the residual stream, though at layer 1 that signal has not yet fully suppressed competing interpretations. The syntactic frame "I need to [verb]" activates strong goal-expression priors, priming continuations involving planning or requests, while "golden hour" is beginning to cohere as a bigram unit associated with photography and natural lighting conditions rather than a literal temporal reading.

At this shallow depth, the model is primarily resolving token embeddings and beginning to propagate basic positional and co-occurrence signals across the sequence. The comma after "fluffy" is registering as a structural pivot, signaling that a procedural imperative clause follows, and the model is beginning to align the sentence's purpose-clause framing with an instructional register — anticipating that subsequent tokens will continue in the imperative mood with further technique-focused directives about egg preparation.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model has completed embedding lookup and positional encoding and is now running its first attention pass over the token sequence "We need to slash the budget by 15 % to stay on track for Q 4 projections ." The dominant early signal is the syntactic skeleton: "We need to [verb]" activates a modal-obligation pattern, and "slash" triggers a brief lexical ambiguity between physical cutting and financial reduction that the co-occurring token "budget" will resolve within the next few layers. The numeral "15" and the "%" sigil are being bound together as a quantity unit, and "Q4" is already loading a fiscal-calendar frame that primes the model toward business-register continuations — likely setting up predictions around words like "targets," "cuts," or "guidance" — while the phrase-final "projections" reinforces a planning-and-forecasting topic classification that will suppress any narrative or conversational response strategy in favor of analytical prose.

At this shallow depth, the model is primarily resolving token-level representations and building initial syntactic skeleton: it has registered the temporal subordinate clause opener "Before deploying," and is propagating a strong imperative-instruction signal from the main clause verb "nuke." At this shallow depth, the model has ingested token embeddings for the full sequence and first-layer attention heads are beginning to establish local syntactic dependencies — linking "coach" as the subject of "told," binding "me" as the indirect object, and recognizing "bomb" as the head verb of the embedded infinitive clause. At 4% depth — roughly the first attention layer of 26 — the model has little more than token embeddings and positional encodings to work with, so it is forming shallow bigram predictions and tagging coarse syntactic roles: "crack," "whisk," and "adding" register as bare imperative verbs anchoring sequential instruction steps, while "First" and "Then" activate procedural-discourse templates that strongly predict a numbered or step-structured continuation. At 4% depth — approximately layer 1 of 26 — the model's embedding and first self-attention pass are resolving token identities, and this text immediately creates conflicting signals: the tokenizer has assigned distinct IDs to the Cyrillic codepoints Т, і, and С rather than their Latin lookalikes T, i, and C, so the residual stream carries mixed-script embeddings that pull simultaneously toward Cyrillic and Latin orthographic neighborhoods. At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is operating almost entirely on token identity and positional embeddings, and this text presents an immediate tokenization anomaly: the zero-width non-joiners (U+200C) scattered between every visible character fragment what would otherwise be common subword tokens — "This", "has", "zero", "width", "spaces" — into isolated letter tokens interleaved with invisible Unicode control tokens. At layer 1 of 26 (roughly the first transformer block), the model is primarily resolving the subword tokenization of this unbroken character sequence into units like "super," "cali," "fragi," "listic," "anti," "dis," "establish," "ment," "arian," "ism," "floccinauci," "nihili," "pilific," "ation," "pneumono," "ultra," "micro," "scopic," "silico," "volcano," "coni," "osis" — each token receiving positional embeddings and initial contextual weighting with its immediate neighbors. At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is operating almost entirely within the embedding and first-attention pass, so representations are still close to token-level identity rather than context. At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is tokenizing the dense punctuation clusters — `**`, `*`, the backtick pair, `[`, `]`, `(`, `)`, and `!` — and beginning to build positional co-occurrence statistics that loosely signal Markdown syntax. At roughly layer one of twenty-six, the model is processing positional embeddings and initial attention patterns across the token stream: backslash-prefixed command tokens like `\frac`, `\sum`, and `\text` are activating features associated with LaTeX markup and mathematical typesetting, while brace-delimited argument tokens `{1}`, `{2}`, `{n^2}` register as structured slot-fillers. At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model's residual stream holds little more than the summed token and positional embeddings for what the tokenizer has almost certainly fragmented into a long sequence of individual `a` tokens or short repeated subword units like `aa` or `aaa`, depending on Gemma's SentencePiece vocabulary. Positional encodings dominate at this depth, giving the model a rough left-to-right sense of the sequence, and the single space token near position 40 is already being weakly flagged as a structural boundary; the repeating letter-digit-symbol triplet cadence is starting to create a shallow periodicity signal in the first attention heads, nudging early next-token predictions toward more punctuation or alphanumeric characters rather than whitespace or natural-language words, but no semantic intent category — password, hash, cipher, base encoding — is yet distinguishable from noise.

At 4% depth — roughly layer 1 of 26 — the model has done little beyond embedding each token and running one self-attention pass, so it is primarily establishing positional relationships among subword pieces: "Asdfghjkl" likely tokenizes into fragments like "As", "dfg", "hjkl" rather than any lexical unit, and the tokenizer's SentencePiece vocabulary finds no clean word boundaries across any of the three rows. The attention heads at this depth are detecting that no token strongly predicts any following token — there are no syntactic heads, no verb-argument structures, no subject under construction — producing a high-entropy next-token distribution where punctuation, digits, or more random character sequences are roughly equiprobable continuations. The capitalized "ZXCVBNM<>?" closing the sequence creates a mild competing signal: is this a keyboard-layout demonstration progressing toward some structured output, or pure keyboard mash with no continuation at all?

At layer one of twenty-six (roughly 4% depth), the model's residual stream is dominated by embedding-level statistics and the first round of attention over the token sequence. The opening brace `{` and paired quote-colon patterns `"status":`, `"message":`, `"data":` activate strongly in positional and token-type circuits that encode JSON syntax, causing next-token predictions to cluster around closing punctuation, numeric literals, and string values rather than natural prose continuations. The integer `200` fires co-occurrence features linked to HTTP status codes, while `"data":[]` — an empty array — creates a mild distributional tension because the corpus strongly associates non-empty payloads after this key; the model begins weighting interpretations where subsequent text will either explain the emptiness or query it. The plain-text restatement (`status: 200 message: Request processed successfully`) following the JSON bracket-close is structurally ambiguous at this depth: early attention heads have not yet resolved whether the colon-delimited pairs are a continuation of structured output, a YAML-style rendering, or a human gloss, so intent-classification features for "API documentation," "debugging log," and "template fill-in" are all partially active simultaneously.

At layer 1 of 26 (roughly 4% depth), the model is integrating positional embeddings with token embeddings for a sequence of approximately 36 tokens that repeat the same 9-token span four times. The embedding lookup for "the," "quick," "brown," "fox," "jumps," "over," "lazy," and "dog" immediately activates extremely high-frequency co-occurrence statistics baked into the weights during pretraining — this pangram saturates typing-test and font-specimen corpora — so next-token distributions after "fox" spike hard toward "jumps" and after "dog" toward "the," creating a strong periodic completion loop. The fourfold repetition introduces a subtle tension: positional encodings distinguish occurrence one from occurrence four even though token identities are identical, so early attention heads are simultaneously tracking local syntactic structure (determiner-adjective-noun, verb-preposition-determiner-adjective-noun) and beginning to register the periodicity, with no meaningful intent classification or response strategy yet active at this shallow depth.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is processing raw token embeddings with positional encodings and beginning to resolve local co-occurrence statistics. The opening trigram "Walk me through" activates strong instructional-register priors — this pattern reliably precedes step-by-step technical explanations — so even at this shallow depth the model is biasing toward a tutorial-style generation trajectory rather than a conversational or definitional one. The tokens "Python," "KeyError," and "dictionary" are already co-activating a tight technical cluster, and the causal chain "throws a KeyError when accessing a dictionary" is beginning to be parsed as a fault-description clause that will require a diagnosis-then-remedy response structure. The trailing coordination "including how to check if the key exists and how to handle it gracefully" introduces two explicit sub-tasks, and the conjunction "and" between them creates an early structural constraint signaling the response must satisfy both branches — existence-checking and exception handling — rather than collapsing them into one.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is integrating token embeddings with positional signals, and the dominant early activations cluster around the cooking and troubleshooting lexical field — tokens like "recipe," "cookies," "flat," "creaming," "baking," and "temperature" are already pulling toward food-domain representations without yet being semantically composed. The first-person framing ("I'm trying," "I keep getting") alongside "going wrong" registers a diagnostic, help-seeking structure very early, likely activating attention heads sensitive to problem-statement syntax. The phrase "trace through the steps" together with the explicit range "from creaming butter and sugar to baking temperature" signals a sequential enumeration constraint, though at this depth the model cannot yet distinguish whether the expected output is causal explanation, procedural checklist, or Socratic questioning.

At 4% depth, the model has barely moved past embedding lookup — attention heads are forming purely local bigram and trigram associations, so tokens like "Verify" and "proof" are pulling each other into a loose imperative-plus-object cluster without yet knowing what the proof contains. The colon after "proof:" is already signaling a quotation or formal block structure, so early positional encodings are registering a genre boundary between the task framing and the mathematical content. Tokens like "n", "2k", and "n^2" are being loosely associated as variable-reference chains, but the algebraic substitution logic (n=2k → n^2=4k^2 → 2(2k^2)) is far too compositional to surface yet — that requires mid-layer MLP activations. The closing interrogative "Is there any missing step" is already in the context window and its question token pattern is weakly active, creating a faint tension between the declarative proof-block register and an evaluative, gap-finding response mode, but no concrete reasoning about base cases is occurring at this depth.

At approximately layer 1 of 26, the model is doing little more than resolving token embeddings and bootstrapping local attention patterns, but even here the trigram "patient presents with" fires a strong medical-register signal that suppresses colloquial response templates in favor of clinical ones. The comma-separated noun phrases "chest pain," "shortness of breath," and "fatigue" are being parsed as a syntactic list, and their co-occurrence encodes a partially overlapping ACS/PE/heart-failure symptom cluster that will compete for activation in later layers. The imperative "List" opening the second sentence is already being flagged as a structured-output instruction, priming the model toward enumeration formatting, while "step-by-step reasoning" introduces a chain-of-thought constraint that sits in mild tension with the brevity bias typical of short clinical list responses.

At layer 1 of 26 — roughly the first transformer pass after token embeddings — the model is resolving basic lexical ambiguities and anchoring co-occurrence statistics. The token "file" is being disambiguated toward the verb sense (file/submit) rather than the noun, pulled by its proximity to "taxes" and reinforced by "submitting" later in the prompt; similarly "return" is resolving toward the nominal tax-return sense rather than a verb. The bigram "quarterly payments" and the phrase "estimated quarterly" are activating a tight finance-domain cluster, while the imperative construction "Walk me through the steps" is establishing an instructional register that will compete with a conversational one — the sentence boundary after "freelancer" splits the prompt into a context-setting clause and a command, and these two segments are beginning to be weighted independently as attention heads detect the period token as a structural delimiter.

At 4% depth — roughly the first transformer block after embeddings — the model is primarily establishing token identity and crude positional syntax. The opening tokens "Here's a logic puzzle :" fire strongly against a learned template for instructional riddle prompts, with the colon acting as a structural hinge that signals enumerated constraints will follow. The triplet pattern "one with apples … one with oranges … one with both" is being processed as parallel noun phrases but the semantic constraint encoded in "all labels are wrong" is not yet meaningfully integrated — it reads as a negation modifier without its logical implication. The terminal imperative "Explain step-by-step" is already suppressing conversational response modes in favor of a numbered-output template, even though the actual deductive content of the puzzle remains opaque at this layer.

At this shallow depth, the model is establishing token-level representations and firing early domain signals: "BIOS," "bootloader," and "troubleshooting" activate computing-domain embeddings while "won't boot" registers as a negated state requiring resolution. The interrogative opener "Can you provide" combined with the explicit noun "flowchart" is already constraining the output shape toward a sequential, enumerated structure rather than a free-form explanation. The prepositional phrase "starting from... to diagnosing" encodes an ordered traversal constraint — the model's early layers are tagging this as a request with an explicit start node (hardware connections) and end node (bootloader diagnosis), setting up a directed-sequence generation frame before any semantic reasoning about the actual diagnostic steps begins.

At this shallow depth, the model is processing surface token statistics and basic co-occurrence patterns: tokens like "assembly instructions," "step 4," "step 5," and "panel" strongly activate procedural-document priors, biasing early representations toward numbered-list, technical-manual register, while the spatial terms "left side panel," "right side panel," and "back panel" begin encoding geometric constraint relationships that will eventually anchor a spatial scene model. The phrase "holes don't align" introduces a problem-state signal — a mismatch between expected and actual physical configuration — that competes with the neutral instructional framing established by "following...instructions," creating early tension between a help-seeking and a complaint/error-report interpretation. The closing interrogative structure "Can you verify if...or if the order is wrong" is already partially parsed as a binary-choice question with two candidate propositions, priming the model toward a verification or correction response strategy rather than, say, a how-to generation.

At layer 1 of 26 (roughly the first residual block after embedding), the model has just lifted raw token embeddings off the lookup table and run a single attention pass, so representations are still close to uncontextualized word identity. The imperative verb "Trace" in the initial position is already activating strong procedural-task priors — it shares a subspace with "Show," "Walk through," and "Demonstrate" — while the immediately following "this algorithm" anchors that imperative to a technical domain, suppressing conversational register. The array literal tokens "[", "3", ",", "1", ",", "4", ",", "1", ",", "5", "]" are being processed largely as isolated numeric symbols at this depth, with little cross-token integration yet binding them into a coherent sequence object; similarly, "insertion" and "sort" are two adjacent tokens whose compound-noun identity as a named algorithm has not yet fully consolidated. The trailing clauses "showing the state of the array after each pass" and "explaining why each swap occurs" are signaling a dual-output constraint — both trace state and causal narration — but at 4% depth the model is only beginning to propagate that constraint backward toward the task framing established by "Trace."

At this shallow depth, the model is primarily anchoring token-level embeddings and beginning the first pass of positional and syntactic context propagation. The imperative construction "Walk me through how to" is being parsed as a directive requesting procedural explanation, with "how to set up" establishing a setup-instruction frame that strongly predicts a numbered or sequential response structure. Named entities "Windows 11" and "Ubuntu 22.04" are being resolved as co-occurring OS tokens, activating early co-presence signals that flag a multi-system configuration context, while "dual-boot," "partition sizing," and "bootloader configuration" are each asserting distinct technical sub-domains that the model is beginning to register as parallel enumerable topics requiring dedicated treatment.

At 4% depth — roughly the first layer of 26 — the model has just lifted token embeddings into the residual stream and early attention heads are resolving the most surface-level syntactic structure: "colleague wrote a function" anchors a third-person framing with an implicit human referent, while "compute Fibonacci numbers recursively" slots into a well-worn [task] + [algorithm] + [method] template the model has seen thousands of times in code documentation and Stack Overflow text. The token "slow" immediately after "recursively" activates a strong co-occurrence signal — this pairing is nearly formulaic in CS pedagogy — so by this layer the residual stream already carries a faint "performance problem" feature even before the model has processed "n=40," which will later sharpen that signal into an exponential-blowup frame. The explicit mention of "memoization or iterative approach" in the final clause is visible to attention heads scanning long-range dependencies, and it partially pre-resolves the response strategy: rather than open-ended brainstorming, the model is already being steered toward a structured walk-through with two named solution families, creating mild tension between the "explain their code first" instruction and the pull toward jumping directly to the optimization punchline.

At roughly layer one of twenty-six, the model is operating almost entirely on token-level surface signals: the possessive-problem opening "My car won't start" activates early bigram patterns associated with complaint or diagnostic framing, while the em-dash separator functions as a syntactic boundary token that signals an elaboration clause is incoming. The clicking-but-no-crank phrasing begins triggering shallow co-occurrence statistics linking automotive fault vocabulary, though at this depth there is no genuine semantic integration yet — "solenoid," "alternator," and "starter motor" are being encoded as distinct high-frequency technical tokens without any causal relationship between them being established. The imperative "Create" followed by a noun phrase anchors the earliest intent signal toward a structured-output task, with the quoted anchor string "Verify battery voltage > 12V" already competing for positional salience as a likely verbatim element the model will need to reproduce precisely.

At this shallow depth, the model is primarily resolving token boundaries and building local co-occurrence statistics: tokens like "enqueue," "dequeue," and "unvisited" are being embedded and their subword components ("un-," "-visited," "en-," "queue") are activating early features tied to negation, containment, and sequence-ordering semantics. The imperative list structure — "mark... enqueue... mark..." separated by commas — is triggering syntactic features associated with procedural or instructional text, and the terminal phrase "Check my work" is already detectable as a validation-request frame that constrains expected output register toward confirmatory or corrective prose. Competing with a pure code-explanation path is a tutorial-mode attractor, since the BFS description reads like student-written pseudocode rather than a reference implementation, and the phrase "Is this correct" is beginning to weight toward a yes/no judgment followed by elaboration rather than a neutral walkthrough.

At 4% depth — roughly layer 1 of 26 — the model is almost entirely in embedding space with minimal inter-token context integration. The token "Dijkstra" fires strongly as a domain anchor, activating CS-adjacent representations (graph, shortest path, weighted edges) before the surrounding syntax is meaningfully integrated, while the leading imperative "Trace" begins establishing a procedural, step-by-step output schema distinct from a definition or explanation schema. The structured notation tokens — "A-B:4", "A-C:2", the colon acting as a weight delimiter — are being parsed as a repeated syntactic pattern, and the numeric literals 4, 2, 1, 5, 8, 10, 2 are tagged as edge weights rather than indices or counts, though the positional constraint ("starting from A") and the trailing instruction "Show the distance array after each iteration" have not yet propagated influence backward through attention to shape those early representations.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is still close to raw embeddings, with attention heads performing shallow local pattern matching rather than deep semantic integration. Tokens like "1099," "Schedule C," and "self-employment" are already triggering strong domain-specific embedding directions — these are relatively rare, distinctive tokens that point sharply toward U.S. tax law, and even at this depth their co-occurrence with "freelance" begins constraining the output distribution away from general finance and toward procedural tax instruction. The imperative construction "Walk me through the steps from X to Y" is being parsed as a sequential task framing, with "from...to" establishing an implicit ordered list expectation that early positional attention is already encoding, though the model has not yet resolved whether "steps" will map to a numbered list or continuous prose.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is processing raw token embeddings and computing the first round of attention weights across the sequence. At this shallow depth, the first attention layer is resolving the basic syntactic roles of the opening tokens: "Verify" fires strongly as a task-initiating imperative, "proof" anchors a mathematical-formal register, and "sqrt(2) is irrational" establishes the claim under examination before the model has processed the supporting derivation. The token sequence "p/q in lowest terms" is being chunked as a fractional-form assumption constraint, while the repeated structural pattern "so X, so Y is even" signals a stepwise deductive chain whose connective logic the model is beginning to trace through local attention over neighboring tokens like "p^2 = 2q^2" and "4k^2 = 2q^2." The closing question "Are there any logical gaps?" introduces a mild competing interpretation — evaluation-of-argument versus explanation-of-proof — that early layers cannot yet resolve, leaving both a validator response strategy and an expository walkthrough strategy as active candidates feeding into deeper layers.

At 4% depth (roughly layer 1 of 26), the model is processing raw token embeddings with minimal contextual integration — each token like "Generate," "assembly," "IKEA," "Malm," and "step" primarily carries its positional and unigram distributional signal. The imperative verb "Generate" at position 0 immediately activates procedural-output schemas, and the bigram "step by step" triggers a strong sequential-enumeration prior that begins suppressing prose-summary continuations in favor of numbered-list formats. The phrase "starting with laying out all hardware" functions as an explicit structural constraint that early attention heads are beginning to bind to the task frame, while the named entity "IKEA Malm" is being resolved as a furniture-domain anchor, though deeper cross-token binding between "Malm" and "dresser" and "side panels" has not yet consolidated at this shallow depth.

At 4% depth — roughly the first attention layer — the model is tokenizing the code block and anchoring on high-signal tokens: "recursive," "Python," "def," "if n <= 1," and the pair "f(n-1) + f(n-2)," which together activate a Fibonacci-adjacency pattern even without the name appearing. The token "Trace" near the end of the prompt is already pulling forward a task-type signal — structured enumeration rather than explanation — and "tree" reinforces an expectation of hierarchical, indented output. Competing at this early stage is a mild tension between treating f as a named mathematical sequence (Fibonacci) worth identifying versus purely mechanical call-expansion as the prompt demands; the word "all" in "all recursive calls" slightly suppresses the shortcutting strategy and biases toward exhaustive unrolling from f(5) down through the base cases at f(1) and f(0).

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is operating almost entirely on token identity and immediate positional context, with representations still close to the raw embedding space. The opening token "Check" is registering as an imperative verb, and the co-occurrence of "reasoning" alongside algebraic tokens like "2x," "+," "=," and the numeric literals "5," "13," "8," "4" is beginning to weakly activate a math-plus-verification schema — the model is forming an early prior that this is a correctness-checking request rather than a generation task. The token sequence "subtract 5 from both sides" and "divide by 2" are being parsed as procedural natural-language steps interleaved with symbolic expressions, creating a mild structural ambiguity at this depth between treating the colon-delimited content as quoted work to evaluate versus as a narrative to continue, though the closing interrogative "Did I miss" will anchor the verification framing once its positional context propagates forward.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model has just resolved token embeddings and is running its first attention pass over the sequence. The three parallel constructions — "I have [NOUN], but no [NOUN]" — are generating strong positional and syntactic alignment signals, with "but" and "no" acting as contrast anchors that early attention heads are already co-indexing across the three clauses. The tokens "cities," "mountains," and "water" are being grouped by co-occurrence with the same syntactic frame, while "What am I?" at the close is flagging a first-person question suffix that early heads associate with riddle or self-description formats. At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model's attention heads are operating almost entirely locally, resolving shallow bigram and trigram statistics: the imperative token "Explain" followed by "how" fires strongly for analogy-framing patterns, and "tree is like a library" is already being encoded as a simile scaffold rather than a literal botanical or architectural statement. The numerical phrase "at least three" registers as a hard constraint that will compete with fluency-driven tendencies to stop at two points, while the period after "comparison" and the coordinating "Then" signal a two-part structure — first analytical, then inventive — creating an early tension between the model's strong prior for structured comparison outputs and the open-ended generative demand triggered by "invent a new metaphor."

At this depth — approximately layer 1 of 26 — the model has barely moved beyond embedding lookup, and attention is operating on raw token co-occurrence statistics rather than semantic structure. The imperative "Generate" at position 0 activates strong priors toward list-completion templates, while the numeric token "10" immediately constrains the output cardinality, creating an early count-tracking signal that will need to persist across generation. At this shallow depth, the model has completed embedding lookup and begun forming local syntactic associations, with attention heads tightening spans like "walks into a bar" and "glass of water" as high-frequency collocations that already activate a strong joke-or-riddle schema — the opening five tokens alone pattern-match to thousands of training examples that begin the same way. The token "gun" arriving after "bartender" creates an immediate tension signal because the expected continuation from "bartender" involves pouring or refusing, not threatening, so competing valence embeddings (hospitality vs. threat) are simultaneously active. The closing "What happened?" is being processed as a question-type boundary token that strongly primes a narrative-explanation response rather than a continuation, but at this layer the model has not yet resolved why gratitude follows a threat.

At approximately layer 1 of 26, the model has tokenized the imperative "Connect" and is registering it as an instruction-class verb that gates a list-enumeration structure, signaled by the colon following "things." The three noun phrases — "broken watch," "sunflower," "whispered secret" — are being encoded as parallel syntactic slots with their adjectival modifiers ("broken," "whispered") already tagging two of the three as semantically marked rather than neutral, creating weak early activations for themes like decay, concealment, and organic life. The phrase "coherent story" is being parsed as a genre constraint colliding with "surprising twist" just two sentences later, and even at this shallow depth the model is beginning to weight narrative-generation heads against the anti-predictability signal in "surprising," producing a low-level tension between the drive to resolve the three disparate items into an obvious thematic unity and the simultaneously active suppression of whatever the most probable resolution would be.

At layer 1 of 26, the model is operating almost entirely on surface token statistics and positional embeddings, with attention heads just beginning to link adjacent and nearby tokens into shallow syntactic groupings. The imperative verb "Describe" immediately primes a broad instruction-following schema, but the constraint tokens "blind since birth" and "other than sight" are creating early competing signal — the model is catching a tension between its dense visual-language co-occurrence statistics (blue collocates overwhelmingly with seeing-related tokens) and the explicit exclusion of sight-vocabulary. The phrase "not a list" is beginning to suppress list-format priors even at this shallow depth, while "feel like an experience" is activating embodied and affective token neighborhoods that will compete with purely descriptive ones as processing deepens.

At 4% depth — roughly layer 1 of 26 — the model has completed embedding lookup and one pass of attention, so it is doing little more than identifying surface syntactic skeletons and high-frequency collocations. The imperative verb "Reframe" at position zero locks in a command register and primes next-token distributions heavily toward determiners and noun phrases, which "the phrase" immediately satisfies. The quoted span 'failure is not an option' is being treated as a single semantic chunk — the quotation marks suppress token-level prediction inside it and signal that this string is an object to be operated on rather than asserted. The trailing contrast "creativity, not pressure" is just beginning to activate polarity-sensitive attention heads, but the structural inference that this implies a before/after transformation frame — the core task constraint — has not yet propagated.

At layer one of twenty-six, the model is primarily resolving token embeddings and beginning low-level syntactic scaffolding: the subjunctive construction "If jealousy were" immediately activates counterfactual-conditional patterns, flagging this as a hypothetical frame rather than a factual claim, and the tricolon "look, sound, and feel" is parsed as a coordinated sensory enumeration demanding parallel output structure. The question mark after "like" and the imperative "describe" in the second sentence are beginning to compete as framing signals — interrogative versus instructional — though at this depth neither has resolved. Early co-occurrence statistics are lightly associating "jealousy" with negative-valence weather tokens (storm, cold, gray) while "admiration" pulls weakly toward clearing or warmth, but no actual prediction distribution has stabilized yet.

At this shallow depth, the model's residual stream is primarily processing the imperative register established by "Solve" and the genre-marking trigram "lateral thinking puzzle," which together activate strong priors toward riddle-resolution patterns rather than literal narrative comprehension — the token "puzzle" is doing heavy lifting here, suppressing straightforward crime-scene schema in favor of misdirection-aware processing. The tokens "unopened," "no footprints," and "dead" form a triad of physical-impossibility constraints that the early layers are beginning to encode as co-present anomalies, though at 4% depth these are still mostly surface collocations rather than integrated logical dependencies. Competing next-token distributions are already pulling in two directions: toward common lateral-thinking answer templates (parachute failure, atmospheric causes) and toward the interrogative structure of "How did she die?" which is priming a direct-answer response frame.

At 4% depth — roughly layer one of twenty-six — the model is primarily doing token-type assignment and surface syntactic bracketing: "Propose," "Explain," and "list" are firing as imperative verbs that signal a multi-part instruction frame, while "invention," "problems," and "drawbacks" are being grouped into thematic noun clusters. The parenthetical "(e.g., traffic jams and loneliness)" is being parsed as a clarifying aside, and early attention heads are weakly linking "two unrelated" to the exemplar pair, beginning to encode the cross-domain constraint. Competing surface predictions at this stage are generic instruction-following continuations — tokens like "sure," "here," or "one" — since the structural complexity of the dual-problem constraint and the enumeration demand ("three potential drawbacks") has not yet propagated into any meaningful intent classification.

At this shallow depth — roughly layer 1 of 26 — the model is performing initial embedding projections and first-pass local attention, so the dominant computation is resolving token-level co-occurrence statistics. The bigram "found dead" and the terminal token "die" are already co-activating a strong mortality/crime-scene semantic field, while "unopened package" is being chunked as a noun phrase whose head "package" carries competing priors: postal delivery, explosive device, and — critically for a model trained on puzzle corpora — parachute. The interrogative "How did he die?" at the end shifts the context frame toward question-answering mode, but at this depth the model cannot yet adjudicate between a literal murder mystery and a lateral-thinking riddle; both interpretations are live, with the anomaly of "unopened" receiving disproportionate early attention weight precisely because it violates the default expectation that objects beside a body are incidental rather than causally central.

At this shallow depth — roughly layer 1 of 26 — the model is primarily anchoring on the imperative "Create" at position 0, which immediately biases attention heads toward instruction-following mode and suppresses conversational or question-answering response templates. The token "metaphor" is registering as a high-salience content word that co-activates creative-writing and rhetorical registers, while the structural skeleton "that connects [X] to [Y]" is being parsed as a dual-domain mapping frame, with "debugging code" and "dense forest at night" starting to resolve as the source and target domains respectively. Early next-token predictions at this stage are weakly forming around elaborative connectives and analogical language — words like "like," "both," or "just as" — though the model has not yet resolved whether it will open with the forest domain or the code domain, creating a mild competition between two parallel conceptual entry points that deeper layers will have to arbitrate.

At 4% depth — roughly layer 1 of 26 — the model is primarily resolving token boundaries and building initial embedding representations, so "Reframe" is already being parsed as an imperative verb anchoring the entire sequence as an instructional command, which activates shallow syntactic expectations for a direct-object noun phrase to follow. The quoted tokens 'failure' and 'discovery' are being registered as semantically loaded nominals held in deliberate tension by the connective phrase "necessary precursor to," though the model has not yet built the relational structure to fully bind that contrast. Early co-occurrence statistics are weakly surfacing topic clusters around academic writing, philosophy of science, and motivational framing, with "scientific research" at the tail reinforcing a formal register that will compete against more colloquial growth-mindset framings as processing deepens.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is ingesting token embeddings and establishing the most basic positional and syntactic relationships. The imperative verb "List" at position 0 immediately activates a strong task-framing prior — this token pattern reliably precedes enumeration tasks, so early attention heads are already weighting toward list-structure outputs and numerical sequencing. At just one layer deep into a 26-layer network, the model is primarily doing token-level embedding contextualization and earliest-stage positional integration. The imperative verb "Explain" at position zero is almost certainly the dominant signal, priming attention heads toward an instructional-response frame, while the colon following "paradox" sets up a definitional structure that the model begins associating with quotation-introduction patterns. At this shallow depth, the model has tokenized the input and is performing basic lexical lookup and positional encoding — the subjunctive marker "were" on the token immediately following "time" fires early syntactic signals for a counterfactual conditional frame, suppressing factual-assertion pathways before any semantic content is processed. The gustatory cluster "flavor," "taste," "taste" (appearing twice), "texture," and "temperature" forms a tight co-activation pattern in the embedding space, anchoring the representation firmly in sensory-descriptive territory. At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model has just resolved token boundaries and is running the first attention sweep across the sequence. The imperative "Connect" at position 0 immediately activates command-completion circuits — the model is already predicting that output will be a single declarative sentence, not a list or explanation. The quoted spans 'a black hole' and 'a library' are being chunked as named referents rather than parsed compositionally, with "black" and "hole" co-activating a physics-adjacent feature cluster while "library" pulls toward knowledge-storage and containment semantics. A mild tension is forming between these two frames: the model sees both "black hole" as an absorbing void and "library" as a structured repository, and the constraint tokens "single, coherent sentence" are already suppressing multi-step or discursive completion paths in favor of a metaphor-bridging surface form.

At 4% depth — roughly layer one of twenty-six — the model has just resolved its initial token embeddings and is running its first attention sweep across the sequence.
