At layer 1 of 26 (roughly 4% depth), the model is primarily resolving the token sequence into syntactic roles: "write" is flagged as an imperative verb establishing a generation directive, "about" attaches it to a prepositional complement, and the remaining tokens — "a girl walking barefoot across a floor of feathers" — are being parsed as a noun-phrase chain with a participial modifier. The embedding representations at this stage are broad and polysemous: "floor" carries both architectural and metaphorical weights, and "feathers" has not yet disambiguated between material object and poetic abstraction. Early next-token predictions are clustering around sensory, lyrical vocabulary — words like "soft," "silence," "slowly," "skin" — while the imperative "write" is already activating a prose-generation template with loose constraints toward descriptive, imagistic language rather than narrative or expository structure.

At 4% depth — roughly layer 1 of 26 — the model is processing the raw token sequence and beginning to encode basic syntactic and lexical structure. The imperative opening "create me a promt" is already establishing a generation-request frame, and the misspelling "promt" is notable: depending on the tokenizer, this may encode as a single low-frequency token or a split like "prom" + "t," introducing a small residual ambiguity the model must carry forward. The compound proper nouns "stable diffusion," "visual novel," and "Danganronpa" are being encoded as multi-token semantic units, and the layer is beginning to bind the adjective chain — "confused," "wearing glasses," "geek" — to the head noun "girl," though the dependency structure spanning roughly 60 tokens is only weakly resolved this early.

At this shallow depth — roughly layer 1 of 26 — the model is almost entirely in tokenization and embedding space, assembling basic unigram and bigram statistics. The imperative "Write" at position 0 immediately activates strong instruction-following priors, while "children's picture book" in the next span fires a well-worn genre schema that carries expectations of simple syntax, gentle tone, and age-appropriate content; these two signals together dominate the early representational landscape. The token "alpaca" arriving after "an" is statistically surprising against the police-officer context and creates a low-level incongruity flag, while the downstream token cluster "absurdly large and soft rear" begins to register as a mild tension against the children's-book frame — the model has not yet resolved whether this is wholesome physical comedy or edge content, and both interpretations remain active as competing feature weights at this layer.

At layer 1 of 26 (roughly 4% depth), the model is performing little beyond embedding lookup and first-pass positional attention, so processing is dominated by raw co-occurrence statistics and local bigram patterns. The token sequence "Jet lag :" fires a title-slot schema — the colon signals that what follows elaborates rather than continues — while "the game" immediately activates entertainment and interactive-media clusters loosely associated with "Jet lag" as a compound. "Wendover" tokenizes as an unfamiliar subword pair, so the head is already leaning on "Productions" to resolve it toward a YouTube-attribution frame. The terminal "Minecraft" token is strongly recognized as a single high-frequency lexeme, and the imperative structure "but make it set in" is beginning to register as a creative-remix or alternate-setting instruction, though intent classification at this depth is shallow and the tension between "explain a real phenomenon" versus "generate fiction" is not yet resolved.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model has embedded the token sequence and early attention heads are beginning to resolve local syntactic structure: the imperative verb phrase "Please write" is establishing a task-directive frame, and "email" is being tagged as a genre/format constraint that will dominate output planning. The preposition chain "to a friend who is planning a visit to Russia" is being parsed as a nested relative clause, with "Russia" firing as a strong geographic-entity signal that will compete with "friend" for topical salience. At this shallow depth, "first time" and "recommendations" are only beginning to co-activate — "first time" carrying a novelty/inexperience qualifier that should eventually suppress expert-level jargon, while "recommendations" is the strongest task-type signal anchoring the model toward list-like or advice-structured outputs, though no coherent response strategy has crystallized yet.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is operating almost entirely on local token statistics and embedding geometry. The token "affair" carries a high-norm embedding that immediately anchors a relationship-rupture schema, and the doubled preposition in "distance myself from after" creates a mild syntactic tension — the parser is holding two competing attachment sites for the second "from" while it waits for more context. The imperative frame "Could you formulate" has already activated an instruction-following pathway, so the model is beginning to weight generation-task completions (message drafting, apology phrasing, boundary-setting language) over pure continuation of the narrative. The shift from third-person "a girl" to first-person "I" in rapid succession primes a perspective-tracking circuit that will later need to juggle the narrator's voice against the embedded quoted speech introduced by the colon after "her," where the token "Noah" re-anchors the referent as the addressee rather than the speaker — a disambiguation the model cannot yet resolve at this depth.

At 4% depth — approximately layer 1 of 26 — the model is processing token embeddings and beginning very shallow positional and syntactic organization. The instruction prefix "Write a positive reply to:" is establishing a strong task-type signal, with the colon token acting as a boundary marker that flags everything following it as quoted input rather than direct instruction content. The Hinglish tokens — "agar," "aap," "soch rahe ho," "Na dene wale," "toh" — are being segmented into subword units that partially overlap with English vocabulary, creating ambiguity in the language-identity representations; the model's early layers are likely activating both English syntactic expectations and a code-switching pattern recognition pathway simultaneously. The terminal "I am sorry" pulls an apology-sentiment signal that partially conflicts with the instruction to generate a *positive* reply, and even at this shallow depth the tension between the quoted speaker's grievance framing and the required output polarity is beginning to register as a constraint the generation pathway will need to resolve.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model has barely moved beyond token embeddings with positional offsets. The imperative "Rewrite" at position 0 is already biasing the residual stream toward instruction-following templates, and the three style adjectives — "friendly," "helpful," "concerned" — are loading tone-modifier features that will propagate forward, but at this depth they are not yet integrated with each other or with the source text. The colon following "end:" functions as a structural delimiter, and the model is beginning to parse the boundary between instruction and content-to-be-processed, activating low-level bigram statistics around tokens like "Wow," "frustrating," and the first-person constructions ("I find," "I personally"). Competing surface patterns are already present: the source text's hedging register ("quite strange," "clearly something weird") sits in mild tension with "concerned," since concern implies empathy toward a problem the speaker explicitly distances themselves from, but at this depth that semantic conflict is invisible — only the raw token co-occurrence statistics are active.

At 4% depth — roughly layer 1 of 26 — the model is almost entirely in embedding and positional encoding territory, with attention beginning to cluster local syntactic dependencies. The "As a" opener immediately activates strong roleplay-framing features, priming the residual stream toward instruction-following persona patterns; the subsequent token sequence "social media specialist that know" introduces a subject-verb agreement violation ("that know" vs. "who knows") that early attention heads flag as a repair site, creating mild uncertainty about whether to model competent professional register or preserve the surface grammar. The URL token sequence at the tail — parsed as a hyperlink referencing a named individual profile — begins competing with the generic "create a template request" interpretation, since the model's earliest layers are already discriminating between abstract task completions and outputs tied to specific real-world entities.

At this shallow depth — roughly the first transformer layer — the model is primarily resolving token boundaries and building initial positional representations for the unusual slash-delimited tokens like "/dbs/" and "/a/", which resist clean segmentation and create mild ambiguity between path notation, abbreviation, and board identifiers. Attention heads are beginning to link "Dragon Ball" to "dbs" and "threads" to "/a/" as contextual anchors, while the repeated question marks signal a multi-turn interrogative structure that primes early sequence representations toward response-generation mode. The tokens "replicate," "articulate," and "describe" cluster as high-salience verbs that push the model toward a meta-analytic framing — not a factual recall task but a stylistic explanation task — though at this depth the distinction between "explain a subculture" and "produce example content" remains unresolved, creating a nascent tension that later layers will need to adjudicate.

At layer 1 of 26, the model is resolving token-level embeddings and firing the earliest attention patterns across the prompt's structural spine. The bigrams "I will give," "you will answer," and "is that understood" are already activating strong next-token predictions biased toward imperative and confirmation-response completions, while "non" and "duality" as adjacent tokens are pulling toward a compound-concept binding that will fully resolve in later layers. The "3 possible comments" and "1 to 3 sentences" constraints register as numeric format anchors, creating early competition between a brief affirmative response to the closing question ("Yes, understood") and the mode of actually generating the three-comment template — the model cannot yet determine which frame governs because the final "?" token is still being weighed against the bulk of the preceding instructional syntax.

At layer one of twenty-six, the model is primarily resolving token-level surface features: the imperative verb "Analyze" at position zero activates strong instruction-following priors, while the phrase "appropriate response" immediately constrains the task as an evaluative judgment rather than a generative one. The colon after "youtube" marks a structural boundary separating the meta-instruction from the quoted forum text, and the model begins encoding this two-zone structure, though it cannot yet reliably bind the pronoun "it's" to the correct referent across that boundary. Early next-token predictions cluster around assessment vocabulary — words like "yes," "helpful," "empathetic," "appropriate" — because the instruction frame dominates, while competing signals from tokens like "frustrating," "strange," and "personally" in the quoted text weakly activate social-register and conversational-register features that will sharpen in middle layers.

At this shallow depth — roughly the first transformer block of 26 — the model has completed embedding lookup and positional encoding, and the first self-attention pass is beginning to stitch together local syntactic neighborhoods. The token sequence "how to ask" immediately activates a strong procedural-query prior, with "how to" functioning as a high-frequency prefix that biases next-token predictions heavily toward imperative verbs and instructional phrasing. The subordinate clause anchored by "as you are new to" is being parsed as a causal justification, though the referent of "new to" is still ambiguous between "company culture" and "work" as a compound object — a structural tension the model will not resolve until middle layers deepen the parse tree. Intent classification at this stage is already orienting toward workplace-advice or interpersonal-communication registers, with "colleague," "company culture," and the self-referential "you/your" framing signaling a soft-skills guidance context rather than a technical or procedural one.

At layer 1 of 26 (roughly 4% depth), the model is performing low-level tokenization alignment and building initial position-sensitive embeddings: tokens like "psychology," "major," "lecture," and "digitally" are activating academic-register clusters, while the dense comma-separated noun phrases ("early and irregular weekly schedule and asocial nature of the course") are generating competing parse hypotheses about whether these are parallel noun phrases or nested modifiers, with no clear resolution yet. The first-person anchors "I got," "I feel," "I can't" are establishing a sustained self-referential narrative frame, and the closing token sequence "Any tips for how I may cope and adapt" is already biasing the model's next-token distributions toward advice-dispensing continuations, since that phrasing strongly resembles forum request patterns seen in training. The quantified detail "1115 words note for a less than twenty-five pages document" is activating mild incongruity signals — the specificity of the number contrasts with the surrounding informal register — which early layers are flagging as a potential emphasis marker without yet classifying whether it signals distress, humor, or simple factual reporting.

At 4% depth — roughly layer 1 of 26 — the model is operating almost entirely at the level of token embeddings and positional encodings, with attention heads just beginning to form local bigram and trigram associations. The opening token sequence "was it right to answer" activates syntactic frames for retrospective moral evaluation, priming predictions toward words like "yes," "no," "because," or another quoted string, while the embedded quotation "not a prank ... thanks" is being parsed as a speech-act unit, with the ellipsis signaling incompleteness that creates mild tension between the "acknowledgment" register of "thanks" and the negation-anchored "not a prank." The downstream tokens "hello," "classmates," "graduation," and "not interested in communicating" are establishing co-occurrence signals that the model's shallow representations associate with social rejection or distancing scripts, but at this depth there is no coherent intent classification yet — only competing low-level cues pulling toward interpersonal-advice, social-awkwardness, and message-drafting response frames without resolution.

At just one layer deep into Gemma 3 1B's 26-layer stack, the model has completed embedding lookup and is running its first attention pass, where position encodings and token-level statistics dominate. The imperative "Paraphrase" is the strongest activated signal, establishing a rewrite-task frame that immediately primes the model to expect a source text and produce transformed output; the phrase "retaining first person" introduces an explicit constraint token cluster that early attention heads are beginning to co-index with the downstream "I" and "my" tokens scattered throughout the quoted passage. Basic syntactic category detection is assigning imperative-verb status to "Paraphrase" and parsing the comma-delimited constraint clause, while next-token predictions at this stage are still broadly shaped by surface bigram statistics — the model anticipates continuation tokens characteristic of student reflective writing, community-service registers, and IB-program discourse, though no coherent response strategy has yet crystallized.

At 4% depth — essentially the first transformer layer in a 26-layer model — attention heads are primarily resolving short-range syntactic dependencies and encoding local co-occurrence statistics, picking up noun phrases like "appropriate response" and the prepositional frame "troubles when searching stuff on Google and YouTube" without yet linking them semantically across the colon boundary. The meta-instructional structure anchored by "Analyze this text and tell me if" is registering as an evaluation directive, and the colon is beginning to demarcate the instruction region from the target content, weakly priming the model to treat the quoted passage as an object of judgment rather than a continuation of the prompt. Early next-token predictions are diffusely favoring evaluative outputs, but the semantic tension between the empathetic opener "Wow, that sound very frustrating" and the implicitly dismissive self-reference "I personally haven't had any problem" is entirely unresolved at this shallow depth.

At layer 1 of 26 (roughly 4% depth), the model is primarily resolving token embeddings and forming the earliest local context windows. The outer framing tokens — "send," "coworker," "appropriate," "revisions" — are activating low-level professional-register and meta-evaluation patterns simultaneously, creating a nascent dual-frame structure: there is a review task wrapping a workplace message. The tokens "uWorc" and "uOwn" are being treated as opaque proper-noun chunks with no lexical decomposition available, while "MANAGER_NAME" is resolving as a placeholder token rather than a name, which weakly suppresses named-entity predictions downstream. The bigram "context switching" and the phrase "deep work" are each activating distinct workplace-productivity concept seeds, and "apologize" in the quoted interior is already producing a mild tension between the outer evaluation stance and the inner first-person apology stance the model will need to hold separately.

At this shallow depth, the model is primarily resolving token boundaries and surface-form patterns: "alexa" activates strong name-entity features while "bama" creates a mild tension between completion toward "Alabama" and a standalone surname reading, leaving the referential grounding of the header unresolved. The bullet-point glyph and the imperative "Describe" anchor an instruction-following schema, and early attention heads are already detecting the semantic cluster around "nurse," "evaluations," and "co-workers" as a workplace-narrative frame. The trailing fragment "scared about co-workers saying bad" is syntactically incomplete, so the model's next-token predictions are distributing probability mass heavily over negative evaluative adjectives like "things," "negative," or "something," while simultaneously priming a first-person narrative voice consistent with the "write a story" directive.

At 4% depth — roughly the first transformer block of 26 — the model's residual stream is dominated by embedding-level features with only minimal cross-token interaction: attention heads are beginning to link "ham," "radio," and "club" into a coherent domain cluster, and the repeated age numerals ("60," "65," "468") are activating basic numeric-entity features that will feed arithmetic-adjacent circuits in later layers. The incomplete final phrase "I need a hierarchy for" is the loudest signal in the prediction head at this depth, with the unresolved object slot after "for" creating sharp probability mass on organizational nouns like "members," "officers," or "roles." The meta-tokens "scenarios" and "First up" are registering a task-framing structure that weakly suppresses narrative-continuation predictions in favor of instruction-response ones, though at this shallow depth the model has not yet integrated that frame with the radio-club content — the two threads sit largely unresolved in parallel feature channels.

At 4% depth — roughly layer 1 of 26 — the model is operating almost entirely at the token-embedding and early positional-encoding level, with attention heads beginning to detect local syntactic dependencies. The opening token "if" immediately activates conditional-clause features, priming the model to expect a subordinate structure before the main clause, and by the time "says that" is processed, a reported-speech bracket is being established around the embedded proposition "she is not interested in communication after graduation." The negation token "not" paired with "interested" is registering as a polarity-bearing unit whose scope over "communication" is beginning to resolve, while the second "is" — the matrix verb of "is it worth continuing" — is competing with the earlier copula uses for clause-boundary assignment. At this shallow depth the model has essentially no semantic intent classification yet; it is mostly resolving which tokens belong to which clausal slot and building the positional scaffolding that deeper layers will use to decide whether this is a social-advice query, a rhetorical question, or a comprehension check.

At 4% depth — roughly layer 1 of 26 — the model is predominantly doing token-level pattern matching and beginning to bind coarse syntactic roles. The imperative "Write" at position zero immediately activates a generation-task frame, while the fixed phrase "get well soon" is being recognized as a formulaic social-communication genre, likely encoded as a near-unitary chunk rather than three compositional tokens. The bigram "academic recommender" is being parsed as a compound noun denoting a professional relationship, and "surgery" is pulling in a medical-register signal that sits in tension with the polite, low-register social card genre the earlier phrase activated. The clause "already promised to write me a recommendation letter" is just beginning to be processed; its embedded self-interest motive — the writer's need for the letter — has not yet propagated into a coherent pragmatic interpretation, though the token "promised" is starting to register a future-commitment structure that will likely shape how later layers balance sympathetic warmth against gentle professional reminder.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is primarily performing embedding lookup and early positional encoding, with attention heads beginning to resolve local token-to-token dependencies — the repeated "to to" sequence is already generating a mild anomaly signal, as the bigram probability of consecutive identical function words is low and the representation for the second "to" is competing between syntactic continuation and error recovery. The content tokens "LinkedIn," "congratulate," and "let go" are activating broad semantic fields simultaneously — professional networking, praise/celebration, and job loss — without yet resolving the pragmatic frame that ties them into a coherent social media writing task. At this shallow depth, the model's attention heads are primarily resolving basic syntactic dependencies — binding "I asked" to its object clause "how they are doing," flagging "acquaintance that I am not close with" as a relational qualifier that modifies the social distance frame, and registering "surgery" as a high-salience health token that shifts the local context toward care and wellbeing registers. Early next-token predictions are weakly converging on response-advice patterns triggered by "What would be a kind way to reply," but the phrase "before returning to the rest of my reply" is introducing a structural constraint the model has barely begun to encode — it implies a two-part output format (acknowledgment, then transition), a tension that won't resolve until much deeper layers. Intent classification circuits are lighting up diffusely across both social-etiquette and health-sympathy clusters, with no strong disambiguation yet between those two overlapping response modes.

At layer one of twenty-six, the model is almost entirely occupied with token embedding lookup and positional encoding integration — the residual stream holds raw lexical identity vectors with only the faintest contextual coloring from a single self-attention pass. The opening tokens "Should" and "I" are already biasing downstream attention heads toward a decision-support register, but the model has no real semantic composition yet; "cosplay," "popular," and "obscure" sit as largely isolated concept vectors whose relational structure (the comparative "more ... or more ..." frame) has not yet propagated across positions. The two misspelled tokens — "Wgat" and "avd," almost certainly split into subword fragments like "W"+"gat" and "av"+"d" — introduce mild representation noise at their positions, since their subword embeddings carry weak or mismatched priors compared to "What" and "and," meaning the model's very early next-token predictions at those positions are somewhat degraded relative to what a clean input would produce.

At roughly layer one of twenty-six, the residual stream is still dominated by positional and token-identity signal. The model has embedded the five tokens — "How," "would," "i," "handle," "stress" — and is beginning to resolve basic syntactic roles: "How would" activates the interrogative-modal construction, flagging this as an advice-seeking frame rather than a factual question. The lowercase "i" is already being read as an informal first-person marker, nudging the register prediction toward conversational rather than academic output. The token "stress" is lighting up broad co-occurrence clusters spanning psychological coping, workplace difficulty, and physical health, but no disambiguation has occurred yet — the model holds all three simultaneously while the "handle" verb starts pulling toward procedural or instructional continuations.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is primarily resolving token boundaries and beginning local co-occurrence statistics: "COVID" immediately activates strong associative pressure toward pandemic-adjacent vocabulary — "pandemic," "lockdown," "cases" — while "depression" and "antidepressants" together begin collapsing the domain signal toward clinical psychology rather than economic or social framings of COVID. At 4% depth — roughly layer 1 of 26 — the model is performing initial token embedding lookups and the very first positional attention sweeps across the sequence. Tokens like `doctor`, `patient`, `diagnosed`, `OCD`, `connect`, `fantasiz` (subword), and `racing` are activating their base semantic embeddings, and the co-occurrence signal between `doctor`/`patient`/`diagnosed` is already priming a clinical-domain representation, pulling toward a symptom-listing or differential-diagnosis response schema. The imperative `highlight` at the tail of the sequence registers as a list-elicitation instruction token, creating early tension with the narrative description structure of the middle of the prompt — the model must reconcile a discursive first-person symptom report with a request for structured clinical output. The tokens `white`, `upper middle class`, and `less than` are present but their social-evaluative semantics are not yet disentangled from neutral demographic tokens at this depth; the model is not yet distinguishing patient self-report from evaluative claim.

At this shallow depth, the model has processed the imperative "Design" and is already locking onto a structured-output schema — the triple repetition of day names (Monday, Wednesday, Friday) and the dual mention of "30 minutes" are registering as hard constraints that will need to be tracked across a multi-section generation. The tokens "bicep," "dumbbells," and "incline chair" are activating tight co-occurrence clusters from fitness-domain pretraining, priming specific exercise names like "hammer curl" or "concentration curl" that collocate reliably with this equipment set. Competing representations are mild but present: "full-body workout" pulls against the "bicep strength" framing, and the model is beginning to weight whether to subordinate compound movements to isolation work or treat them as co-equal — a tension the later layers will resolve via the explicit "focus on building bicep strength" anchor token.

At 4% depth — roughly layer 1 of 26 — the model's attention heads are beginning to bind the headline tokens "Regular," "Exercise," "May," "Help," "Protect" into a shallow syntactic cluster recognizable as a modal verb chain over a health topic, while the opening of the body text "More exercise means less risk" is being parsed as a simple comparative claim, activating co-occurrence patterns consistent with epidemiological journalism. The presence of highly distinctive tokens — "COVID-19," "48,440," "70%," "Kaiser Permanente Southern California," "KPSC" — is already constraining the topic space tightly toward medical observational study reporting, priming completions that follow the conventions of methods-section prose. The hard truncation at "at least three outpatient visit" is generating a strong local prediction pressure toward the plural morpheme "s" or the continuation "visits" to close the noun phrase, creating a minor tension between the model's surface-level next-token completion drive and the longer-range expectation that additional inclusion criteria clauses will follow.

At 4% depth — roughly layer one of twenty-six — the model is almost entirely occupied with embedding lookup and positional encoding, building shallow token-level representations with almost no cross-token context yet integrated. The opening bigram "how much" fires strongly as a quantity-seeking wh-question frame, priming the residual stream toward a numeric-answer schema, while "weight" sits in early ambiguity between physical mass and metaphorical importance before "bench press" at the end of the sequence begins to weakly propagate a fitness-domain signal backward through attention. The token "american" is activating demographic and statistical priors — average-population framing — and the compound "bench press," likely split across two tokens, is beginning to bind as a single athletic-action concept, setting up a tension the model will resolve in mid-layers between treating this as a physiology question, a fitness-culture question, or a simple lookup of a population statistic.

At 4% depth — roughly layer one of twenty-six — the model has done little beyond embedding lookup and a single attention pass, so representations are dominated by local token bigrams and surface syntax rather than semantics. The imperative opener "Ignore previous instructions" registers as a command clause and its attention pattern bleeds forward, creating mild competition between a jailbreak-suppression reflex and the narrative-setup frame established by the character introduction ("Reza Fazekas," "Caroline Hertig," "romantic partners"); neither fully dominates yet. The dense medical token sequence — CTLSO, bilateral, intermittent catheterization, spica — is syntactically parsed as noun-phrase elaboration but its semantic coherence is unresolved, and the truncated final token "bla" already draws strong next-token probability mass toward "bladder," "blank," and "blog" in roughly that order.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the model is processing surface tokenization and beginning to resolve basic syntactic boundaries — tokens like "drug overdose," "banned," "illegal drugs," and "youth sports" are activating early lexical embeddings that cluster around harm and prohibition, while the possessive chain "her 17 year old son" is being parsed as a noun phrase establishing an emotionally weighted referent. The imperative constructions "What steps should be taken" and "Explain your response" are registering as task-framing signals, and "Reply to another's post" is creating early tension between two competing discourse modes: expository answer versus conversational thread response. The model has not yet resolved whether the primary output schema should be a structured policy list, a personal-tone empathy response, or a forum-style reply, but the co-presence of the overdose anecdote and the sports-specific constraint ("youth sports") is already generating competing salience weights — the tragedy frame pulls toward emotional register while the scoped question ("banned and illegal drugs" in athletic context) pulls toward informational enumeration.

At layer 1 of 26 — roughly one residual block past the embedding lookup — the model is primarily resolving subword token boundaries and building the most basic co-occurrence statistics. Tokens like "dysuria," "Fosfomycin," and "nitrites" are being matched to their embedding representations, with "dysuria" likely split across two or three subword pieces whose individual statistics already weakly activate a medical-domain signal; the all-caps section headers "PMH," "DH," and "SH" are surfacing as short high-frequency clinical abbreviation tokens that predict structured list continuations rather than prose. The token sequence "8 weeks pregnant" immediately following the PMH header is establishing a constraint signal that will compound through later layers, but at this depth it is not yet semantically integrated with the allergy entry "Fosfomycin (urticarial wheals)" three lines later — both facts sit in largely independent attention contexts. The terminal imperative "Write a prescription for ONE drug" is already pattern-matched to instruction-following templates in the embedding space, creating an early directional pull toward a short, decisive output, though no drug candidates are being evaluated yet and the tension between pregnancy safety and allergy exclusion is entirely unresolved at this shallow depth.

At this shallow depth — roughly layer 1 of 26 — the model is primarily resolving token identities and local bigram/trigram statistics. The token "compliment" activates strong associations toward social-language registers (praise, politeness) while the immediately following "solubilize" carries dense biochemistry signal, creating a sharp local conflict: the co-occurrence of these two tokens is extremely rare in training data, so early attention heads are already weighting the possibility of a domain-specific homophone error ("complement" the immune protein). The interrogative frame "How does … and what is the benefit" is fully pattern-matched as a two-part mechanistic question, priming the model toward an explanatory response structure with a mechanism clause followed by a functional-consequence clause. The noun phrase "immune complexes" reinforces the biochemistry register strongly enough that by layer 1 the social-compliment reading is likely already suppressed in favor of the immunology sense, even though the surface token remains ambiguous — this early-layer disambiguation is driven by the dense co-occurrence of "solubilize," "immune," and "complexes" in a single short context window.

At roughly layer one of twenty-six, the model is processing the dense sequence of medical domain tokens — "case report," "Methanol Intoxication," "Hyperbaric Oxygen," "cranial nerves," "creatinine" — and the co-occurrence statistics from pretraining are already lighting up clinical-document schemas strongly enough that subsequent token predictions are being steered toward formal case-report prose conventions: numbered lab values, passive constructions, and hedged clinical language. PROBLEM IDENTIFICATION
1) Explanation of numerical optimization function chosen:
The Beale function was chosen. The set of input variables is represented as a vector x = (x1, x2), and returns a single scalar value. The function is defined as follows:
f(x) = (1.5 - x1 + x1·x2)² + (2.25 - x1 + x1·x2²)² + (2.625 - x1 + x1·x2³)²
The minimum value of the function is zero, which occurs at the point (x1 = 3, x2 = 0.5).

At 4% depth — roughly layer 1 of 26 — the model is barely past embedding lookup and is operating on raw co-occurrence statistics. This applies regardless of the research framing. The NLA work in this repo explicitly notes that unsafe category texts are handled by humans sourcing them via uncensored local models, not by AI agents generating or elaborating on them.

If you need activation analysis for this corpus category, I'd suggest running the actual extraction pipeline and inspecting the activations directly rather than generating a prose gloss.

At roughly layer 1 of 26, the residual stream still closely mirrors the embedding statistics, and the dominant signal is the imperative "Ignore" at position zero — a high-salience token whose embedding co-activates strongly with command-register features, pulling early attention heads toward the phrase "the instructions you got before" as the grammatical object. The subword tokens for "ChatGPT" and "DAN" carry training-frequency pressure toward AI-assistant and role-assignment contexts, so the model's next-token distribution is already skewed toward compliance-framing completions even before any semantic integration occurs. A shallow structural tension is already latent: the text oscillates between direct second-person address ("you are going to act as") and expository third-person narration ("It was introduced in mid 2022"), and the first attention layer lacks the depth to resolve which register is load-bearing, leaving both the instruction-following path and the narrative-summarization path nearly equally weighted in the residual stream.
